reason: The text describes Egyptian oppression of the Hebrew people, not Hebrew leaders raging against their own people. The passage depicts Pharaoh (the Egyptian king) becoming fearful of the Israelites' growth and initiating harsh labor and infanticide policies against them. In contrast, the Hebrew midwives (Shiphrah and Puah) are shown refusing to comply with the Pharaoh's orders to kill Hebrew boys, and Hebrew families work to protect their children. There is no depiction of Hebrew leaders raging against the Hebrew people.
